title: dalbergia gracilis
intro: Dalbergia gracilis, also known as the Philippine rosewood or Molave, is a deciduous tree species native to the Philippines. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dalbergia gracilis include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia gracilis include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
